note: Honest coverage frontier. status: present = a runnable step drives real code and passes (must name a test); pending = specified behavior the parent will bind (REQ-501 scenarios bind against core/auth; the rest against core/httpapi, core/ingest, core/audit, core/schema, core/persist as they land); retrospective_gap = shipped without a driving test (must be zero for governed behavior). The REQ-501b generated-contract scenario is tagged @REQ-501b and mapped to its parent REQ-501, the interface requirement it hardens.

scenarios.name: An unauthenticated stats request is rejected before body parse
A route registered with auth=none fails to register at boot
A replayed nonce is rejected
A stale-timestamp request is rejected
A tampered body invalidates the HMAC signature
Session-replay mints a new gated workflow from a read-only snapshot
A replay lookup for an unknown id returns not-found
An id the caller has no authority over is indistinguishable from a missing id
Generated wire contracts cover every routed endpoint with provenance
An ingest adapter publishes a triage.requested event keyed by external_ref
Ingest runs dedup, flap, burst, and correlate before publishing
Every risk classification appends one session_risk_audit row to the ledger
Every governed row is stamped with its writer's schema_version
A reader rejects a row written by a future schema version
The discovered_scheduled_reboots registry is bi-temporal with a kill switch
The escalation_queue is append-only and re-enters the gated pipeline
Registering a route with auth set to none panics at registration
A verifier without replay protection fails closed at construction
An operator login issues a read-only session that satisfies the read surface
A session principal never satisfies a machine route and never writes
Tampered, expired, and revoked session cookies are rejected before the handler runs
Operator login is rate-limited per operator and source ip
The sessions surface serves the audit spine over an operator session
An unwired sessions surface fails closed instead of fabricating rows
An accepted ingest appears on the alerts surface with its validated fields
A rejected payload never reaches the alerts surface
The governance surface reports the posture its components hold
The secrets surface carries references and resolution only, never a value
The events stream emits the live posture on connect
The models surface relays the gateway report verbatim and fails closed when unwired
The contract surface serves the generated endpoint map verbatim
The estate surface serves the latest published graph snapshot
The estate surface reports no snapshot rather than fabricating a topology
The grounding scorecard publishes the verifier distribution and the falsifiability signal
The grounding scorecard reports honest zeros over an empty spine
An authenticated operator vote signals the waiting decision it answers
A vote on a closed decision window reports conflict, never success
The config surface reports each knob with its source and the LAW clamp holds at resolve
Step-up elevation grants the admin write tier on the same session
The admin tier fails closed when unconfigured and refuses non-elevated callers uniformly
A config write to a LAW key is refused as unprocessable — the clamp is the law
An accepted config write is ledgered before the override row commits
A sealed secret round-trips through envelope encryption and refuses a wrong key or name
The secret write surface is write-only and the read surface lists names without values
The mutation gate exposes a runtime disable that keeps mutation off
The safety metrics exposition renders mutation_enabled zero and the breaker gauge
